newsSince: No material new company-specific news since June 9. The defining facts are unchanged: SkyWater is being acquired by IonQ at $35.00/share ($15.00 cash + $20.00 IonQ stock, collar-set exchange ratio), implying ~$1.8B equity value, with an FTC Second Request (issued April 24) extending the HSR review; both companies still target a Q2/Q3 2026 close. At $36.40 the stock sits modestly above the $35 reference, reflecting IonQ-stock appreciation lifting the stock component and/or deal-close optimism.

sources.label: FTC Second Request extends SkyWater-IonQ review (StockTitan 8-K)

verdicts.rankRationale: On an alpha screen rewarding hidden, asymmetric CPO/advanced-packaging upside, SKYT no longer fits: it is a pending IonQ takeover at a $35 reference ($15 cash + $20 IonQ stock). Standalone foundry/CPO upside is capped/converted into a merger-arb-plus-IonQ-equity bet, while an unresolved FTC Second Request leaves deal-break downside. With the stock at $36.40 (above reference), asymmetry stays inverted vs the page's thesis.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: IonQ deal close (Q2/Q3 2026) with rising IonQ stock lifting the stock-component value above $35.
verdicts.primaryRisk: FTC Second Request / antitrust block; IonQ share decline shrinking the stock component.
verdicts.bull: Deal closes; IonQ-stock leg appreciates, paying above the $35 reference - low standalone risk.
verdicts.neutral: Now a merger-arb/IonQ-equity proxy, not a clean specialty-foundry alpha bet.
verdicts.bear: FTC could block or force remedies; a deal break or IonQ-stock fall reprices toward standalone value.

verdicts.rankRationale: On the test-time-scaling/sovereign-AI-supply-chain lens, US specialty-foundry leverage (Fab 25, 400k wafer-starts/yr) is a real strategic fit. But the name is now a pending IonQ acquisition with an open FTC Second Request, which converts it from a standalone scaling-supply story into a deal-arb situation and caps independent upside. Hold rank with a small negative tilt for the regulatory overhang.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Sovereign US foundry capacity (Fab 25) feeding domestic AI/quantum supply chains.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Pending IonQ merger + FTC Second Request caps standalone scaling thesis; deal uncertainty.
verdicts.bull: Scarce US pure-play specialty foundry with expanded capacity, strategically vital to sovereign AI supply.
verdicts.neutral: Strong sovereign-supply fit, but the IonQ deal/FTC review now drives the equity, not fundamentals.
verdicts.bear: Standalone scaling thesis is moot under a takeover; FTC review adds deal-break overhang.
